_meta.description: NEQ (Numéro d'entreprise du Québec) registration through the Registre des entreprises du Québec. Required for all businesses operating in Quebec.

title_en: Register Your Business with the REQ (Get Your NEQ Number)
title_fr: Immatriculation au Registre des entreprises (Obtenir votre numéro NEQ)
plain_language_summary: The NEQ (Numéro d'entreprise du Québec) is your Quebec business ID number. Almost every business operating in Quebec must register with the Registre des entreprises du Québec (REQ) to get one. You need your NEQ before you can register for taxes, apply for permits, or open a business bank account. It's your first step.
mandatory_note: Required for all businesses, including home-based and sole proprietorships operating under a trade name. If you operate under your exact legal name (e.g., 'Jane Smith Consulting'), registration is technically optional but strongly recommended.

who_must_register: Any business using a name other than the owner's full legal name
Any business applying for government permits or funding (NEQ almost always required)
who_may_be_exempt: Individuals operating strictly under their full legal name with no trade name

steps.title: Choose your business structure and trade name
steps.detail: Decide if you're a sole proprietor, partnership, or corporation. Choose your trade name (the name you'll operate under). The REQ will check for name conflicts.

steps.title: Register online at registreentreprises.gouv.qc.ca
steps.detail: Create an account and complete the registration form online. You'll need: your SIN (for sole proprietorship), your home address, your business address, and a description of your activities.

steps.title: Pay the registration fee
steps.detail: Pay $41 by credit card online. Processing is immediate online.

steps.title: Receive your NEQ number
steps.detail: You receive your 10-digit NEQ number immediately online, or within 1-3 business days by mail. This number goes on all your invoices, contracts, and permit applications.

notes: Registration fee is $41 (2026 — verify current amount at REQ website)
Annual update (declaration annuelle) is $36 — you must file this every year or your registration is cancelled

what_you_need: Social Insurance Number (SIN) — for sole proprietorship
Full legal name and home address
Business address (can be home address for home-based businesses)
Trade name (the name you want to operate under)
Description of business activities (NAICS code — REQ helps you find the right one)

what_you_get.public_listing: Your business appears in the public REQ directory

common_mistakes: Thinking you don't need to register because you're small or home-based — if you use a trade name, you must register
Forgetting the annual update ($36/year) — your registration gets cancelled after non-filing
Not updating the REQ when your address or activities change — updates are required within 30 days of a change
Using a trade name that's too similar to an existing registered name — the REQ will reject it

next_steps: Register with Revenu Québec for provincial taxes
Register for GST/QST if revenue will exceed $30,000
Open a business bank account (bring your NEQ certificate)
Apply for sector-specific permits using your NEQ

languages.note: REQ website and services are available in both official languages
